title: 〜なくてもいい
description: 〜なくてもいい means "don't have to" / "there's no need to." It's the negative counterpart of obligation — telling someone an action isn't necessary.

content.text: Take the verb's ない-form, drop the final い, and add くてもいい. 行（い）く → 行（い）かない → 行（い）かなくてもいい ("don't have to go"). Literally it reads "even if you don't do it, it's fine," which is exactly the nuance: the action is optional, so feel free to skip it.

content.japanese: verb ない-form（drop い）＋ くてもいい
明日（あした）は来（こ）なくてもいいよ。
content.english: You don't have to come tomorrow.

content.japanese: 駅（えき）が近（ちか）いので、運転（うんてん）しなくてもいい。
content.english: The station is close, so I don't have to drive.

content.japanese: この本（ほん）は今日（きょう）返（かえ）さなくてもいいです。
content.english: You don't have to return this book today.

content.japanese: 心配（しんぱい）しなくてもいいよ。

content.text: Variants: 〜よい・〜かまわない・casual 〜なくていい

いい can be swapped for the more formal よい or for かまわない ("it doesn't matter") with the same meaning. In casual speech the も is often dropped, giving 〜なくていい. And when the clause continues, いい becomes the て-form よくて — as in "you don't have to drive, and…"

content.japanese: 〜なくてもいい ＝ 〜なくてもよい ＝ 〜なくてもかまわない
この辺（へん）は便利（べんり）で、運転（うんてん）しなくてもよくて、店（みせ）も近（ちか）い。
content.english: This area is convenient — you don't even have to drive, and the shops are close too.

content.japanese: 疲（つか）れているから、あまり考（かんが）えなくてもいい映画（えいが）が見（み）たい。
content.english: I'm tired, so I want to watch a movie I don't have to think much about.

content.text: 〜なくてもいい is the direct opposite of 〜なければならない ("must do"). If someone asks 行（い）かなければなりませんか？ ("Do I have to go?"), the reassuring answer is いいえ、行（い）かなくてもいいです ("No, you don't have to"). Don't confuse it with 〜てもいい, which grants permission to do something rather than excusing you from it.

examples.japanese: verb ない-form（drop い）＋ くてもいい
明日（あした）は来（こ）なくてもいいよ。
examples.english: You don't have to come tomorrow.

examples.japanese: 駅（えき）が近（ちか）いので、運転（うんてん）しなくてもいい。
examples.english: The station is close, so I don't have to drive.

examples.japanese: この本（ほん）は今日（きょう）返（かえ）さなくてもいいです。
examples.english: You don't have to return this book today.

examples.japanese: 心配（しんぱい）しなくてもいいよ。
〜なくてもいい ＝ 〜なくてもよい ＝ 〜なくてもかまわない
この辺（へん）は便利（べんり）で、運転（うんてん）しなくてもよくて、店（みせ）も近（ちか）い。
examples.english: This area is convenient — you don't even have to drive, and the shops are close too.

examples.japanese: 疲（つか）れているから、あまり考（かんが）えなくてもいい映画（えいが）が見（み）たい。
examples.english: I'm tired, so I want to watch a movie I don't have to think much about.

headings.text: Variants: 〜よい・〜かまわない・casual 〜なくていい
